ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers vegetable juices and nectars; this product is a sweetened vegetable juice drink with concentrate, which is the closest defensible FoodEx2 category. No exact code for sweetened vegetable juice drink with added sugar exists in the published FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for sweetened vegetable juice drink; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one glass), equivalent to 250 g, providing about 72.5 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Vegetable juice drink — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database, record D03070037, with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of vegetable juice — analogous estimate based on low-GI vegetable juice beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 15 is an analogous estimate based on low-GI vegetable juice beverages documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. Vegetable-based juices with low sugar density and moderate fibre consistently fall in the low GI range (GI 10–20). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (15 × 6.81 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 2.6; the input record states GL 1.0 per 100 g serving basis, which is preserved as provided. GL per 250 ml serving recalculated as approximately 2.6 but input value of 1.0 per 100 g basis is retained in this field per input record.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 5.78 g sugar per 100 g and 146 mg sodium per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage: the 5.78 g/100 g sugar content classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), which independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, and the 146 mg/100 g sodium adds further cardiovascular burden — choose unsweetened 100% vegetable juice instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar at 5.78 g/100 g in liquid form with rapid glycaemic absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this drink: despite a low estimated GI of 15, the 5.78 g/100 g added sugar in liquid form is rapidly absorbed without the fibre buffering of whole vegetables, and regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with worsened glycaemic control and increased insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage classification and elevated sodium (146 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease, this beverage is not recommended: the combination of added sugar (5.78 g/100 g, classifying it as an SSB) and moderate sodium (146 mg/100 g) contributes to dyslipidaemia, elevated blood pressure, and increased CVD risk — replace with plain water or unsweetened vegetable juice.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This processed sweetened vegetable juice drink is not suitable for rheumatoid arthritis management: added sugar at 5.78 g/100 g promotes inflammatory cytokine activity, and the product lacks omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or sufficient whole-food fibre (only 1.94 g/100 g) to confer anti-inflammatory benefit — choose freshly pressed unsweetened vegetable juice instead.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegetable juice drink is a processed beverage made from vegetable juice concentrate reconstituted with water and sweetened with added sugars or sweeteners. It is typically sold in cartons or bottles and consumed chilled as a snack or light refreshment. The flavour is mildly savoury-sweet with a thin, watery texture, lacking the body and fibre of whole blended vegetables. Per 100 g, it provides 29 kcal, 0.29 g protein, 0 g fat, 6.81 g carbohydrate (of which 5.78 g is sugar), 1.94 g dietary fibre, and 146 mg sodium. The fat content is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a fat-free beverage by design. Calcium and iron are reported as 0.0 mg per the HPB Singapore database, consistent with the dilute vegetable concentrate base. Despite a low estimated glycaemic index of approximately 15, the product is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g per 100 g in liquid form. This SSB classification makes it unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease, as liquid sugars are absorbed rapidly and SSBs independently raise blood pressure and insulin resistance risk. The moderate sodium level (146 mg/100 g) adds further concern for hypertensive individuals. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Whole vegetables or unsweetened 100% vegetable juice are clinically preferable alternatives.
common_user_queries: Is vegetable juice drink high in sugar?
Is vegetable juice drink good for high blood pressure?
Is sweetened vegetable juice bad for you?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record D03070037) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database category for low-GI vegetable juice beverages, documented as an estimate.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — this is a fat-free beverage by design, with 0 g fat confirmed per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D03070037). saturated_fat_g is therefore also 0.0 by formulation. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 mg and iron_mg as 0.0 mg per HPB source; these are confirmed zero values consistent with a dilute vegetable concentrate beverage. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific sweetened vegetable juice drink are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database record D03070037. GI value of 15 is an analogous estimate based on low-GI vegetable juice beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database; no exact GI test result for this specific product was identified. GL of 1.0 is per 100 g serving basis as provided in the input record.
